examples.title: Your Weight
examples.content: You weigh 70 kg. That's a scalar. Just a number. No direction. You don't weigh 70 kg north or downward—just 70 kg.

examples.title: Room Temperature
examples.content: The room is 22°C. Temperature is a scalar. You can't have temperature pointing in a direction. It's just 22 degrees.

examples.title: Adding Flour to a Recipe
examples.content: You add 2 cups of flour, then 3 more cups. That's 5 cups total. Scalars add simply: 2 + 3 = 5.
